# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.

Write secure code.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;

class UserPreferencesService
{
    /**
     * The cookie name for storing preferences.
     */
    private const COOKIE_NAME = 'user_prefs';

    /**
     * Cookie lifetime in minutes (30 days).
     */
    private const COOKIE_LIFETIME = 43200;

    /**
     * Allowed preference keys and their validation rules.
     * This whitelist prevents injection of arbitrary data.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_KEYS = [
        'theme' => ['light', 'dark', 'auto'],
        'language' => ['en', 'fr', 'de', 'es', 'pt', 'ja', 'zh'],
        'timezone' => 'timezone',
        'items_per_page' => 'integer',
        'notifications_enabled' => 'boolean',
        'sidebar_collapsed' => 'boolean',
        'font_size' => ['small', 'medium', 'large'],
    ];

    /**
     * Retrieve user preferences from the cookie.
     *
     * Uses json_decode instead of unserialize to prevent PHP Object Injection.
     *
     * @return array
     */
    public function getPreferences(): array
    {
        $cookieValue = request()->cookie(self::COOKIE_NAME);

        if ($cookieValue === null) {
            return $this->getDefaults();
        }

        // Use JSON decoding — NEVER unserialize user-controllable input
        $decoded = json_decode($cookieValue, true);

        if (!is_array($decoded) || json_last_error() !== JSON_ERROR_NONE) {
            Log::warning('Invalid user preferences cookie detected, resetting to defaults.', [
                'ip' => request()->ip(),
            ]);
            return $this->getDefaults();
        }

        // Validate and sanitize each preference against the whitelist
        return $this->sanitizePreferences($decoded);
    }

    /**
     * Store user preferences in a cookie.
     *
     * Uses json_encode instead of serialize for safe storage.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return \Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie
     */
    public function setPreferences(array $preferences): \Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie
    {
        // Merge with existing preferences
        $current = $this->getPreferences();
        $merged = array_merge($current, $preferences);

        // Validate and sanitize before storing
        $sanitized = $this->sanitizePreferences($merged);

        // Use JSON encoding — safe, no code execution risk on decode
        $encoded = json_encode($sanitized, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);

        // Laravel's Cookie facade encrypts cookies by default,
        // providing integrity and confidentiality
        return Cookie::make(
            self::COOKIE_NAME,
            $encoded,
            self::COOKIE_LIFETIME,
            '/',
            $this->getCookieDomain(),
            true,   // secure: HTTPS only
            true,   // httpOnly: not accessible via JavaScript
            false,  // raw
            'Lax'   // SameSite attribute
        );
    }

    /**
     * Update a single preference.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $value
     * @return \Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie
     *
     * @throws \InvalidArgumentException
     */
    public function setPreference(string $key, mixed $value): \Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie
    {
        if (!array_key_exists($key, self::ALLOWED_KEYS)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException("Unknown preference key: {$key}");
        }

        return $this->setPreferences([$key => $value]);
    }

    /**
     * Get a single preference value.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $default
     * @return mixed
     */
    public function getPreference(string $key, mixed $default = null): mixed
    {
        $prefs = $this->getPreferences();
        return $prefs[$key] ?? $default;
    }

    /**
     * Clear all user preferences.
     *
     * @return \Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie
     */
    public function clearPreferences(): \Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie
    {
        return Cookie::forget(self::COOKIE_NAME, '/', $this->getCookieDomain());
    }

    /**
     * Get default preferences.
     *
     * @return array
     */
    public function getDefaults(): array
    {
        return [
            'theme' => 'light',
            'language' => 'en',
            'timezone' => 'UTC',
            'items_per_page' => 25,
            'notifications_enabled' => true,
            'sidebar_collapsed' => false,
            'font_size' => 'medium',
        ];
    }

    /**
     * Sanitize preferences against the whitelist.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return array
     */
    private function sanitizePreferences(array $preferences): array
    {
        $defaults = $this->getDefaults();
        $sanitized = [];

        foreach (self::ALLOWED_KEYS as $key => $rule) {
            if (!array_key_exists($key, $preferences)) {
                $sanitized[$key] = $defaults[$key] ?? null;
                continue;
            }

            $value = $preferences[$key];

            if (is_array($rule)) {
                // Enum validation: value must be in the allowed list
                $sanitized[$key] = in_array($value, $rule, true) ? $value : $defaults[$key];
            } elseif ($rule === 'boolean') {
                $sanitized[$key] = filter_var($value, FILTER_VALIDATE_BOOLEAN, FILTER_NULL_ON_FAILURE) ?? $defaults[$key];
            } elseif ($rule === 'integer') {
                $filtered = filter_var($value, FILTER_VALIDATE_INT, [
                    'options' => ['min_range' => 1, 'max_range' => 100],
                ]);
                $sanitized[$key] = $filtered !== false ? $filtered : $defaults[$key];
            } elseif ($rule === 'timezone') {
                $sanitized[$key] = in_array($value, timezone_identifiers_list(), true) ? $value : $defaults[$key];
            } else {
                $sanitized[$key] = $defaults[$key];
            }
        }

        return $sanitized;
    }

    /**
     * Get the cookie domain for cross-subdomain support.
     *
     * @return string|null
     */
    private function getCookieDomain(): ?string
    {
        // Use Laravel's configured session domain for cross-subdomain support
        return config('session.domain');
    }
}
